commit.message:
Add post-assembly validation step for tool-family articles (#115)

* Add post-assembly tool-family validation

Add a post-assembly validator that checks tool count integrity and tool-to-section cross-references before a tool-family article is treated as complete. The validator also reports non-blocking prompt/header/annotation issues and is invoked automatically at the end of Step 4 to catch silent omissions like the foundryextensions threads-get-messages drop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add integration tests for 5-Validate-ToolFamily.ps1

Tests cover blocking checks (tool count, cross-reference, duplicates, missing frontmatter) and warning checks (required params, example headers, annotation markers).

commit.message:
docs: update safeoutputs write-sink examples for all guard policy options

Update README and config.example.toml to document that write-sink is
required for ALL output servers when DIFC guards are enabled, not just
for scoped repos. Add examples showing correct accept values for every
GitHub guard policy option:

- repos="all" / repos="public" → accept=["*"] (wildcard)
- repos=["owner/*"] → accept=["private:owner"]
- repos=["owner/repo"] → accept=["private:owner/repo"]
- repos=["owner/prefix*"] → accept=["private:owner/prefix*"]

README changes:
- Rewrite write-sink section to lead with "required for ALL" emphasis
- Add separate JSON/TOML examples for scoped vs broad access
- Add "*" wildcard as first entry in accept format list
- Update "When to use" to say required, not optional

config.example.toml changes:
- Add "IMPORTANT: Write-sink is REQUIRED" note
- Show both wildcard and scoped accept examples
- Add quick reference table mapping repos → accept

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add wildcard accept ["*"] for write-sink guards

Add WildcardTag ("*") support to DIFC label subset checks. When the
superset side of a flow check contains "*", the check passes regardless
of what tags the other side has.

This enables write-sink accept=["*"] for repos="all" and repos="public"
configurations where the agent has no secrecy tags but still needs a
write-sink to prevent noop guard integrity violations.

Changes:
- Add WildcardTag constant and wildcard logic to checkFlowHelper,
  SecrecyLabel.CanFlowTo, and IntegrityLabel.CanFlowTo
- Update ValidateWriteSinkPolicy to accept ["*"] as sole entry
- Reject "*" mixed with other accept entries
- Update WriteSinkAcceptRules godoc and WriteSinkGuard docstring
- Update README accept rules table: repos="all"/"public" → ["*"]
- Add 20+ tests: DIFC wildcard flow checks, write-sink evaluation
  with wildcard accept, and validation edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add unit tests for mcp.NormalizeInputSchema

Add internal/mcp/schema_test.go with comprehensive unit tests for the
NormalizeInputSchema function in schema.go. Previously this function
had no test file in the mcp package itself; coverage only existed
through integration tests in the server package.

New tests cover all 8 branches of the normalization logic:
- nil schema → default empty object schema
- empty schema (no type, no properties) → empty object schema
- no type with properties → add "type": "object" (copy)
- non-string type value → returned as-is
- non-"object" type string (e.g. "string", "array") → returned as-is
- object type with properties → returned as-is
- object type with only additionalProperties → returned as-is
- object type missing both properties and additionalProperties → add empty properties (copy)

Additional tests verify:
- Extra fields ("required", "description") are preserved when a copy is made
- Original schema is never mutated in copy-producing branches
- Returned map is a distinct reference from the original
- Various toolName variants do not affect normalization logic
- Deeply nested schemas are returned without modification

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add drug pipeline docs, scripts, categories

Add content/schema documentation and a small data pipeline for drug records. Includes docs/CONTENT_SCHEMA.md and docs/DATA_PIPELINE.md describing the JSON schema, taxonomy, and pipeline workflow. Add two utilities: scripts/split_drugs.py (one-time split of flat drugs.json → per-category files) and scripts/build_drugs.py (merge category files into a master drugs.json with validation, dry-run and --exclude-deprecated support). Also add numerous per-category JSON files under src/data/drugs/categories/ and update src/data/drugs.json (master) to reflect the rebuilt dataset. The scripts include duplicate detection, required-field warnings, deprecation handling, and orphan detection to keep the master in sync with category sources.

Co-Authored-By: Copilot <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix annotation spacing: preserve blank line after label (#108)

* Fix annotation spacing: preserve blank line after label

Problem: AI improvement stage was removing the blank line between
'[Tool annotation hints]:' label and the annotation values.

Root cause: ProtectTemplateLabels regex matched label line with
\s*$ pattern (trailing whitespace to end-of-line), which did not
include the newline after the label. When AI processed the content,
it collapsed the blank line that followed the protected token.

Fix: Changed regex from \s*$ to \s*\r?\n to explicitly capture
the newline after the label, so the entire line including its
terminator is preserved as a unit during AI processing.

Result: Annotation section now renders correctly with proper spacing:
[Tool annotation hints](index.md#tool-annotations-for-azure-mcp-server):

Destructive: ❌ | Idempotent: ✅ | ...

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add regression tests for annotation spacing fix

Tests validate that blank lines after template labels are preserved
during protect/restore roundtrip. Coverage includes:

- Annotation hints label
- Example prompts label
- Required/Optional parameters labels
- Prerequisites label
- Multi-label documents
- Windows (\r\n) and Unix (\n) line endings
- Labels without blank lines

10 new tests verify the fix in PR #108 where the regex pattern
changed from '\s*\$' to '\s*\r?\n' to explicitly capture the newline
character, preventing blank line collapse during AI processing.

Total suite: 42 tests passing.

Related PR: #108 - amos/fix-annotation-spacing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add tool description quality rules to generation prompts (#109)

* fix: remove keywords YAML frontmatter from generation prompts

The keywords meta tag is not an industry-standard SEO field - Google
stopped using it in 2009 and Bing ignores it. Removing from tool family
and horizontal article generation to simplify output.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add prompt rules: convert --switch to natural language, strip LLM guidance

Updated tool description improvement prompts with two new rules:

1. Convert --switch-name references to natural language equivalents
   (e.g., --resource-group -> 'resource group') in description text,
   unless the switch name is a parameter keyword in the parameter table.

2. Remove LLM-specific tool-calling guidance from descriptions
   (e.g., 'Use this tool when...', 'Do not use this tool to...',
   'equivalent to az ...', tool disambiguation hints). The docs are
   for people using Azure MCP Server from their IDE, not for LLMs.

Files changed:
- ToolGeneration_Improved/Prompts/system-prompt.txt (new section 6)
- ToolGeneration_Improved/Prompts/user-prompt-template.txt (items 10-11)
- ToolFamilyCleanup/prompts/tool-family-cleanup-system-prompt.txt
- ToolFamilyCleanup/prompts/tool-family-cleanup-user-prompt.txt
- docs-generation/prompts/tool-family-cleanup-system-prompt.txt
- docs-generation/prompts/tool-family-cleanup-user-prompt.txt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add backtick formatting consistency rule to tool description prompts

Adds a new rule to all 6 tool description improvement prompt files requiring
that terms appearing in backticks in the parameter descriptions table must
also use backtick formatting when referenced in the tool description text.
This ensures visual consistency between description prose and parameter tables.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Scribe: OB-Xd VST3 bridge integration session complete

- Orchestration logs: Spike (architecture), Jet (deployment), Faye (host diagnostics), Ed (verification), Coordinator (integration)
- Session log: Final bridge message-pump refactor validated
- Decision inbox merged: 6 entries covering IPlugFrame fix, versioned deployment, thread affinity root cause
- Cross-agent history updated: Team learnings documented
- Archive check: decisions.md at 152KB, entries all recent (no archiving needed)

Architectural invariant locked:
> The bridge main thread is the UI/STA thread. All window creation, COM operations, plugin loads, and IPlugView calls happen on this thread. Pipe reader is background thread only.

Validation complete:
✅ Shipped Debug bridge byte-matches rebuilt Release bridge
✅ load_ack succeeds; openEditor successfully calls attached() without deadlock
✅ OB-Xd editor incomplete UI confirmed as plugin-side (beyond minimal host scope)
✅ Versioned deployment + manifest working end-to-end
⚠️ Test coverage gap flagged: 0 automated tests currently cover VST3/UI behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: minimal-music-keyboard
repository.description: Lightweight system-tray MIDI player for Windows 11 — WinUI3, MeltySynth, NAudio WASAPI, per-instrument SoundFont

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add `export-audit` CLI command for wallet transaction CSV export (#7700)

Wallet needs to export transactions as a CSV for auditing, with optional
date range filtering and fiat conversion.

## Changes

### `cli.rs`
- New `ExportAuditArgs` struct with: `--output-file`, `--start-date`,
`--end-date`, `--conversion-rate`, `--currency` (default: `USD`)
- New `ExportAudit(ExportAuditArgs)` variant in `CliCommands`
- `parse_utc_datetime` helper accepting RFC3339 and `YYYY-MM-DD` /
`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` formats

### `commands.rs`
- `ExportAudit` handler: fetches all completed transactions, filters by
date range, calls `write_audit_to_csv_file`
- `write_audit_to_csv_file`: emits CSV with columns matching the spec —
transaction type is `Deposit` (inbound/coinbase) or `Withdraw`
(outbound); transaction hash derived from first kernel hash; amounts in
XTM (Minotari); optional fiat column using supplied rate

### `wallet_modes.rs`
- Added `ExportAudit` arm to the exhaustive command-match test

## Example

```bash
minotari_console_wallet export-audit \
  --output-file audit.csv \
  --start-date 2021-01-01 \
  --end-date 2021-12-31 \
  --conversion-rate 3130.0 \
  --currency USD
```

Output CSV columns:
```
ID, Transaction Hash, Status, Transaction Type, DateTime (UTC),
From Address, To Address, Amount, AmountTicker, Amount (USD),
Txn Fee, FeeTicker, Fee (USD)
```

proposes.

select a conversion rate and currency.
> The file should look as follows and be a csv file:
> 
>

From Address | To Address | Amount | AmountTicker | Amount (USD) | Txn
Fee | FeeTicker | Fee (USD) |

Add iTipFooty.com.au auto-tipping integration

Automates submitting prediction engine picks to iTipFooty:

- Login via POST to services/login.php with session cookie management
- Parse tipping page HTML to extract game numbers, team names, form fields
- Map prediction engine picks (team IDs) to iTipFooty H/A selections
- Submit tips via POST to services/SubmitTips.php
- Handle locked games (already kicked off) gracefully

Backend:
- New service: backend/src/services/itipfooty.ts
- New route: /api/itipfooty/status and /api/itipfooty/submit
- Credentials loaded from .env (ITIPFOOTY_USERNAME, PASSWORD, COMP_ID)
- dotenv added for env var loading
- Submission logged to DataSourceLog

Frontend:
- Settings page: iTipFooty Integration card with status display
- Submit Tips button with detailed result feedback per game
- Shows configuration instructions when not configured

Security:
- Credentials stored in backend/.env (gitignored)
- .env.example provided with empty placeholders
- Status endpoint masks username with ***

commit.message:
fix: Use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude (#3100)

* fix: use AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var instead of hardcoded claude

The recipe runner and delegation scripts hardcoded 'claude' as the agent
binary, causing failures when amplihack is run with other agents (copilot,
codex, etc.).

Changes:
- cli.py: Set AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY env var at dispatch for all 5 agent cmds
- rust_runner.py: Forward --agent-binary flag to Rust recipe runner
- platform_cli.py: Read env var instead of hardcoding 'claude'
- delegate_response.py (3 mirrors): Read env var + add missing 'import os'

The Rust recipe runner companion changes are in
rysweet/amplihack-recipe-runner#29.

Fixes #3099

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address review findings - empty string edge case and missed hardcoded files

- Fix orchestrator.py: use ${AMPLIHACK_AGENT_BINARY:-claude} bash fallback
- Fix executor.py (2 locations): same bash fallback pattern for remote execution
- Fix empty string edge case: use 'or "claude"' pattern instead of default param
  in platform_cli.py and all 3 delegate_response.py mirrors

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Ubuntu <azureuser@devr.yb0a3bvkdghunmsjr4s3fnfhra.phxx.internal.cloudapp.net>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add gamification-design skill — codified UX learnings

Captures design principles from building XP/level/streak system:
- 'Felt, not seen' philosophy — rewards amplify, never distract
- Duolingo/Nintendo visual patterns (warm gold, chunky bars, centered)
- Technical gotchas (CSS transitions > Framer Motion for progress bars)
- Explicit colors > theme variables for critical gamification UI
- Anti-patterns and PR checklist

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use explicit amber/gold for XP bar and level badge

Replaced bg-chart-1 theme variable (which wasn't rendering visibly)
with explicit amber-500/amber-400 colors. Warmer, Duolingo-inspired
gold that's guaranteed visible. Track uses bg-white/10 for contrast.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix XP bar not reflecting progress — use inline style over Framer animation

Switched from Framer Motion initial/animate (which can fail to re-trigger)
to a CSS transition with inline style width computed directly from
xpInCurrentLevel / xpToNextLevel. Also removed max-w-xs constraint so
the bar takes full container width.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Clean up Progress page — Duolingo-inspired centered layout

- Level badge: bigger (80px), centered above chunky XP bar (h-4)
- XP label: minimal fraction below bar (450 / 600 XP)

- Stats tiles: centered with justify-center, tighter text
- Session history: compact cards, +XP badge without 'XP' text
- Greeting: bold centered hero with wave emoji
- All padding moved to parent container for consistent alignment

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: batch bulk operations and fix added counter accuracy

Add subtle shimmer sweep on cover art when resuming a session

A light gradient sweeps across the game cover art when transitioning
from Paused to Playing. Felt, not seen — no XP toast, no numbers,
just a visual wink that the game is alive again.

Add Phase 2 DB migration (003_phase2.sql)

- Add status (draft|published, CHECK constraint) and published_at to courses
- Create terms table with tenant-scoped unique code
- Add term_id FK to courses
- Add hidden and position columns to course_modules
- Create course_groups, course_group_members, course_groupings,
  course_grouping_groups, and enrolment_methods tables
  (method_type enforced via CHECK constraint)
- Add FK on enrolments.group_id → course_groups (ON DELETE SET NULL)
- Add four indexes for query performance

All DDL is idempotent via IF NOT EXISTS / DO $$ pg_attribute checks.

commit.message:
feat: add gamification system — XP engine, streaks, Player HUD, and XP toasts

- XP engine (lib/xp.ts): variable XP for sessions, new games, beat games, comebacks
- Streak engine (lib/streak.ts): consecutive day + weekly ratio tracking
- DB migration (scripts/014_gamification.sql): gamification columns on users/sessions
- Server actions: XP awarded on createSession, updateSessionStatus (finish), markGameBeaten
- Retroactive XP backfill for existing users on first load
- Player HUD: sticky RPG status bar with streak fire, animated XP bar, session count
- XP toast: animated +XP loot drop notification via context provider

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Phase 5 — GoDoc, polished README, presentation slides

- Add package-level doc comments to all 5 internal packages
  (engine, experiment, k8s, siem, report)
- Polish README.md: architecture ASCII diagram, badges,
  full Makefile reference, coverage table, proper quickstart
- Create thesis/slides.md: 16-slide presentation deck covering
  motivation, architecture, MTTD, evaluation results, RQ answers,
  test coverage, limitations, future work, and demo script
- Fix go vet: restore missing import block in k8s/client.go

Phase 5 complete. All deliverables done:
  - go vet clean
  - 89.3% test coverage
  - GoDoc on all exported symbols
  - Slide deck ready
